title: MicroPython runtime dependencies must be declared by generate and installed by deploy with mpremote mip
symptoms: Device raises ImportError: no module named 'unittest'
Generated device tests pass static subset checks but fail on real MicroPython firmware
The generated project imports a micropython-lib package but no deploy-time installation plan exists
The agent vendors library source into firmware/lib without a version, source, or license record
wrong_pattern: Assuming every MicroPython documented or micropython-lib module is already present in the target firmware, or silently crawling and copying library source into the project during generate.
correct_pattern: Use route A for device tests: continue using MicroPython unittest, but generate must declare runtime_dependencies.mip and deploy must install missing packages with mpremote mip install before running device tests. Generate should not crawl library source into the project by default.

generate_role: Declare runtime_dependencies.mip entries with package, reason, target, verify_import, and install_phase=deploy.
Record official docs and package index evidence.
Do not install packages on the device.
Do not vendor micropython-lib source by default.
deploy_role: Probe the selected device for required imports.
Run mpremote mip install for missing runtime_dependencies.mip packages.
Persist mip_install_result.json and feed it into deploy_result.
Classify dependency install failures separately from application runtime failures.
exception_paths: If offline deployment is explicitly requested, generate may create a vendored dependency plan with pinned source, license, and commit.
If a dependency is too large or must be frozen, route it to the firmware build or flash-firmware phase rather than normal deploy.
If target firmware lacks enough filesystem space, deploy must stop with action_required instead of hiding the failure.

fix_guidance: If device/tests import unittest, keep unittest but add runtime_dependencies.mip for package unittest.
Do not copy micropython-lib source into firmware/lib unless the user chooses offline vendoring.
Ensure phase_complete exposes runtime_dependencies so upy-deploy-plugin can run mpremote mip install.
Add a deploy-time import probe result to error_context when dependency installation fails.
Document package source as micropython-lib or explicit GitHub package URL.
